page: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for parallel computing workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6226R processor featuring 30 dedicated physical cores and 30 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 2.9 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, utilizing a KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 90.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the server bundles two NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, delivering a total of 64 GB of HBM2 VRAM. Local storage consists of a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional performance in machine learning tasks. The dual NVIDIA V100S GPUs enable top-tier performance in large language model inference, specifically for prompt processing and text generation across 7B and 70B parameter models. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also registers strong performance in the top 25%. Conversely, general-purpose CPU benchmarks, including single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations, fall consistently within the average middle 50% range. This highlights a clear performance tradeoff: while the CPU provides standard compute capabilities, the primary processing power is concentrated in the GPU accelerators.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2-le-90 offers a specialized hardware profile that avoids the premium pricing of newer-generation GPU architectures while still delivering high-density parallel compute resources. The inclusion of dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage provides a stable, non-oversubscribed environment. This server is highly suited for workloads such as deep learning training, LLM inference, scientific simulations, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks. It is less optimal for memory-intensive applications that require high RAM-to-vCPU ratios.
description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the x86_64 architecture using the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6226R processor with 30 dedicated physical cores and 30 vCPUs operating at 2.9 GHz, paired with 90.0 GB of system memory. The server's primary strength lies in its dual NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, which provide 64 GB of total VRAM and deliver top-tier performance in large language model inference and parallel processing tasks. Local storage is supported by a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and network throughput is rated at a 4 Gbps baseline. While general CPU and single-core benchmarks perform in the average range, the server offers a cost-effective, high-density hardware profile for GPU-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and scientific computing.
og_description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 30 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 90 GB RAM, a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA V100S GPUs with 64 GB VRAM for high-performance parallel computing.
meta_description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a KVM server with 30 Intel Xeon cores, 90 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA V100S GPUs optimized for LLM inference and parallel workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and NVMe storage for high-performance parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM
Intel Xeon 6226R processor with 30 dedicated physical cores
500 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference tasks
Best suited for deep learning, scientific simulations, and parallel computing
